structure.title: Preface
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document is the front matter and security declaration section of the CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches Hardware Description manual (Version 200, Issue 13, dated April 22, 2026) published by Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Document Identification**: Title, version, issue number, and publication date of the hardware description document

2. **Copyright and Legal Notice**: Copyright information and restrictions on reproduction or transmission of the document without prior written consent

3. **Trademarks and Permissions**: Statement regarding Huawei trademarks and acknowledgment of other third-party trademarks

4. **Disclaimer and Warranty Notice**: Clarification that products and services are subject to contractual terms, and that information is provided "AS IS" without warranties or guarantees

5. **Security Declaration** covering:
   - Product Lifecycle Policy and End of Life procedures
   - Vulnerability Management and Response Process
   - Initial Digital Certificate Rights and Responsibilities
   - Huawei Enterprise End User License Agreement (EULA)
   - Product Documentation Lifecycle Policy

6. **Company Contact Information**: Huawei's address in Shenzhen, China and website reference

7. **References to Support Resources**: Multiple links to Huawei support pages for warranty, security bulletins, and policy details

structure.title: About This Document
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document is a hardware description guide for the CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches (Version 200). It is intended for network engineers responsible for network design and deployment.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Document Overview** - Intended audience, symbol conventions for hazard warnings and notices

2. **Cabinet Systems** - Introduction to three cabinet models (A610-22, A612-22, A812-20)

3. **Chassis Models** - Detailed specifications for CloudEngine 16804, 16808, and 16816 chassis with naming conventions

4. **Power Supply Systems** - Comprehensive coverage of power supply systems for all three chassis models, including AC/HVDC and DC power modules, power switches, and various power distribution units

5. **Heat Dissipation Systems** - Overview of cooling systems including fan modules and chassis doors for each model

6. **Interface Cards** - Extensive documentation of:
   - Main Processing Units (MPU) with multiple variants
   - Switch Fabric Units (SFU)
   - 10GE, 40GE, and 100GE interface cards with multiple port configurations

7. **Cables** - Detailed specifications for DC power cables, AC/HVDC power cables, ground cables, console cables, Ethernet cables, fiber jumpers, and high-speed cables

8. **Pluggable Modules** - Comprehensive guide to optical and copper modules for various interface types (GE, 10GE, 40GE, 100GE) with specifications and compatibility information

structure.title: Using the Info-Finder
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document section introduces the Info-Finder tool platform, which is designed for enterprise and carrier users to search for key product information organized by product series and model. The main points covered include:

1. **Purpose and Scope**: Info-Finder enables users to access comprehensive product information including software specifications, life cycles, hardware details, and operation/maintenance information such as licenses, alarms, logs, commands, and MIBs.

2. **Hardware-Related Tools**: The document outlines four primary hardware tools available within Info-Finder:
   - **Product Image Gallery**: Provides product photos and network element icons for creating design drawings and networking diagrams
   - **Hardware Configuration**: Automatically generates hardware configuration diagrams and calculates weight, power consumption, and heat consumption based on selected components
   - **Hardware Center**: Offers technical specifications of devices and components, plus mapping relationships between devices, components, and versions
   - **3D Model**: Allows users to query product images, product overviews, and component insertion/removal videos for quick one-stop product information access

The document appears to be part of the CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches Hardware Description manual.

structure.title: Version Requirements for Components
structure.summary: This section outlines version requirements for components in CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches. It provides information about supported device models and modules, directs users to Huawei's website for accurate subscription details, and references product change notices and lifecycle management bulletins. The document notes that figures presented are for reference purposes only and is part of the Hardware Description documentation (Version 200, Issue 13 from April 22, 2026).

nodes.title: Introduction to the A610-22 Cabinet
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

This section of the document provides comprehensive information about cabinet models for CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches:

## A610-22 Cabinet (Primary Focus)
- **Naming Convention**: Explanation of the four-field naming system (model, 600mm width, 1000mm depth, 2200mm height)
- **Compliance Standards**: Adheres to IEC 60297-1 international standards
- **Physical Features**: 
  - Made of class-A high-strength carbon cold rolled steel with galvanized coating
  - RoHS compliant with UL-certified fire-resistant interior materials
  - Adjustable mounting rails (25mm increments)
  - Mesh front and rear doors for airflow
  - Static load capacity of 1 ton
  - 47U available space

- **Key Functions**:
  - Component protection from contamination and damage
  - Overhead or underfloor cabling support
  - ESD jack for grounding during installation/maintenance
  - Efficient heat dissipation through front-to-rear airflow design

- **Technical Specifications**: Detailed dimensions, weights (empty: 135kg, assembled: 215kg), environmental operating parameters (temperature: 0-40°C long-term, humidity: 5-85% RH long-term), and installation requirements

## A612-22 Cabinet
- Brief introduction with naming convention explanation (section begins but continues beyond provided text)

## A812-20 Cabinet
- Listed in table of contents but not detailed in this excerpt

nodes.title: Introduction to the A612-22 Cabinet
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

This document section provides comprehensive technical specifications and descriptions of network equipment cabinets, specifically:

1. **Environment Parameters and Installation Requirements** - Operating temperature ranges (long-term: 0°C to 40°C, short-term: -5°C to +55°C), humidity levels, and static load bearing capacity (1000-1200 kg depending on model).

2. **A612-22 Cabinet Introduction** - Detailed naming conventions explaining the cabinet model designation, dimensions (600mm width × 1200mm depth × 2200mm height), and physical structure components (front door, top cover, rear door, side panels, mounting rails, and rack).

3. **Cabinet Construction and Compliance** - Materials used (class-A high-strength carbon cold rolled steel and galvanized sheet), compliance with RoHS and UL standards, and adherence to IEC 60297-1 international standards.

4. **Features and Functions** - Mesh door construction for airflow, adjustable mounting rails in 25mm increments, component protection capabilities, and support for overhead or underfloor cabling.

5. **Heat Dissipation and Safety** - Airflow design with air intake from front and base, exhaust through rear door, and ESD (Electrostatic Discharge) jack for personnel safety during installation and maintenance.

6. **Technical Specifications Table** - Detailed specifications including part numbers, dimensions with/without packaging, weights, available space (47 U), and installation scenarios.

7. **Introduction to A812-20 Cabinet** - Beginning of specifications for an alternative cabinet model.

# Description of Partial Document

structure.title: Chassis
structure.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

This section describes the chassis components of CloudEngine switches, specifically covering:

1. **Chassis Overview** - Introduction to three CloudEngine models: CloudEngine 16804, CloudEngine 16808, and CloudEngine 16816

2. **Chassis Naming Conventions** - Explanation of the naming scheme used for CloudEngine chassis, including:
   - Identifier "A": Represents the CloudEngine series for data center core switches
   - Identifier "B": Indicates the number of LPU (Line Processing Unit) slots available in the chassis:
     - 04 = four LPU slots
     - 08 = eight LPU slots
     - 16 = sixteen LPU slots

3. **Product Classification** - Clarification that these are part of the CloudEngine 16800 Series data center core switches

The document provides a structured reference guide for understanding how CloudEngine chassis models are named and differentiated based on their LPU slot capacity.

nodes.title: Chassis Naming Conventions
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

This section of the document covers the Chassis component of CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches, including:

1. **Chassis Naming Conventions** - Explains the naming structure for CloudEngine chassis models, breaking down the identifier components:
   - Series designation (CloudEngine)
   - Number of LPU (Line Processing Unit) slots (04, 08, or 16)
   - Power module configuration type (AC & high-voltage DC or DC only)

2. **CloudEngine 16804 Specifications** - Provides detailed information about the CloudEngine 16804 chassis model, including:
   - Two variants: AC & high-voltage DC assembly chassis and DC assembly chassis
   - Part numbers for each variant
   - Model designations (CE16804-AH and CE16804-DC)
   - First supported software versions for both V200 and V300 releases

3. **Installation Safety Caution** - Includes a warning about color-coded labels on cards (SFUs and LPUs) that must match to prevent equipment damage during installation

The document serves as a hardware reference guide for understanding and identifying CloudEngine 16800 series chassis configurations and their specifications.

nodes.title: CloudEngine 16804
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

## CloudEngine 16804 Chassis Overview
- Two chassis variants: CE16804-AH (AC & high-voltage DC) and CE16804-DC (DC only)
- Supported versions: V200R005C20 and V300R020C00

## Physical Specifications
- Dimensions: 438mm x 442mm x 874mm (chassis body) or 438mm x 483mm x 985mm (with brackets)
- Weight: 94.1 kg (base) to 176.4 kg (fully configured)
- Chassis height: 9.8 U
- Cabinet installation standards: A812, A612, A610

## Component Architecture
- **MPU slots**: 2 slots (active/standby 1:1 redundancy)
- **LPU slots**: 4 slots (supports different LPU types)
- **SFU slots**: 9 slots (must be same model, load balancing/redundancy mode)
- **Power modules**: 6 slots (plug-and-play)
- **Fan modules**: 3 slots (hot standby redundancy)

## Power Specifications
- AC: 220V 50/60Hz (176-290V range)
- HVDC: 240V/380V (188-400V range)
- DC: -48V/-60V (-40 to -72V range)
- Maximum output power: 15,000W (AC/HVDC) or 13,200W (DC)

## Environmental & Operational Requirements
- Operating temperature: 0°C to 40°C
- Operating humidity: 5-85% RH (non-condensing)
- Operating altitude: ≤5,000m
- Storage temperature: -40°C to +70°C
- MTBF: 34.93 years
- Availability: 0.9999971695

## Indicator Panel & Controls
- Power, MPU, LPU, SFU, and Fan module indicators (green/red status lights)
- Dual power switches for redundant control

## Brief Introduction to CloudEngine 16808
- AC & high-voltage DC assembly chassis variant mentioned with part number 02352RCV

nodes.title: CloudEngine 16808

# Description of Partial Document

This document section provides comprehensive hardware specifications and technical details for Huawei CloudEngine 16800 Series network switches, specifically covering the CloudEngine 16808 and 16816 models.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Storage Specifications**: Storage temperature range (-40°C to +70°C), storage environment standards (ETSI EN 300 019-1-1 Class 1.2), and one-year valid storage period with proper packing materials.

2. **CloudEngine 16808 Overview**: Two chassis variants (AC & high-voltage DC assembly and DC assembly) with their respective part numbers and supported software versions.

3. **Physical Appearance and Components**: Front and rear view descriptions, component layout including MPUs (Management Processing Units), LPUs (Line Processing Units), SFUs (Switch Fabric Units), power modules (PMs), and fan modules.

4. **Slot Configuration**: Detailed slot layout specifications for LPU slots (1-8), MPU slots (9-10), SFU slots (1-9), PM slots (1-10), and fan module slots (1-3), with operational modes and restrictions.

5. **Panel Indicators and Controls**: Description of LED indicators (PWR, MPU, LPU, SFU, FAN) showing operational status and power switches for device control.

6. **Technical Specifications**: Comprehensive specifications including dimensions, weight, power consumption, heat dissipation, redundancy features, input/output voltage ranges, operating and storage conditions, altitude limits, humidity ranges, and reliability metrics (MTBF, availability).

7. **Power Supply Details**: Information on AC/HVDC and DC power supply modes, redundancy options, and maximum output power configurations.

8. **CloudEngine 16816 Introduction**: Basic information table introducing the 16816 model variants.

nodes.title: CloudEngine 16804 Power Supply System
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

This section of the document describes the power supply systems for Huawei CloudEngine switches (16804, 16808, and 16816 models). The key topics include:

1. **Power Supply System Overview** - Introduction to the power supply architecture supporting DC, AC, and HVDC power inputs with dual power inputs (A and B) for redundancy.

2. **CloudEngine 16804 Power Supply System Details** - Comprehensive coverage including:
   - Power supply configuration options (single or dual power sources)
   - Automatic failover mechanisms and load-balancing modes
   - Power module (PM) and power entry module (PEM) components and their functions
   - Power switch functionality for controlling PM output

3. **Power Supply Scenarios and Applications** - Mapping of different power supply types (DC, AC, HVDC) to corresponding PEM and PM configurations with specific wattage ratings.

4. **N+1 Backup Mechanism** - Explanation of power redundancy configuration with detailed tables showing PM connections, PEM connections, and rated current requirements based on overall power consumption levels.

5. **Power Supply Architecture** - Detailed descriptions and diagrams of:
   - DC power supply architecture
   - AC & HVDC power supply architecture
   - PDU (Power Distribution Unit) integration options

6. **AC & High-Voltage DC PEM Specifications** - Overview and appearance details of the integrated PEM module for the CloudEngine 16804.

nodes.title: CloudEngine 16804 AC&High-Voltage DC PEM
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

1. **AC & High-Voltage DC Power Supply Architecture** - Overview of the CloudEngine 16804's power supply system design and architecture

2. **CloudEngine 16804 AC & High-Voltage DC PEM (Power Entry Module)**
   - Pre-installed by default and cannot be removed or replaced onsite
   - Integrated into the chassis with no onsite configuration needed
   - Physical appearance and panel layout details

3. **Power Socket Configuration**
   - Mapping between six PEM power sockets (A1/B1 through A6/B6) and corresponding power modules (1-6)
   - Power socket specifications supporting 200V AC to 240V AC/240V DC/380V DC input
   - Dedicated high-voltage DC terminal specifications (PowerConnector, 3Pin, 450V, 16A)

4. **Input Requirements and Power Distribution**
   - Support for 220V AC input in AC power distribution scenarios
   - Support for 240V/380V high-voltage DC input in DC power distribution scenarios
   - Minimum rated current requirements (16A for UPS, 20A for mains power grid)
   - Protection component requirements for power distribution frames (PDFs)
   - Power distribution box (PDB) configuration requirements with double-line backup support

5. **CloudEngine 16804 DC PEM** - Introduction to the dedicated DC power entry module with similar pre-installed and non-replaceable characteristics

nodes.title: CloudEngine 16804 DC PEM
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

1. **Protection Component Requirements**: Derating requirements for fuses and circuit breakers must be noted when using protection components.

2. **Power Distribution Frame (PDF) Configuration**: Input circuit breakers of a PDF must match output circuit breakers.

3. **Power Distribution Box (PDB) Requirements**: When front-end PDF lacks sufficient power inputs, a PDB must be configured with specific requirements:
   - Minimum output current specifications (16A for UPS, 20A for mains power)
   - Double-line backup support
   - Input current must meet or exceed sum of output line currents

4. **CloudEngine 16804 DC PEM Overview**: 
   - Connects the CloudEngine 16804 to DC power sources
   - Pre-installed by default and cannot be removed, installed, or replaced onsite
   - Integrated into chassis and requires no onsite configuration

5. **DC PEM Physical Details**:
   - Terminal block appearance and layout
   - Location mapping between PEM terminal blocks and Power Modules (PMs)
   - Terminal block descriptions including NEG (-48V/-60V) and RTN (BGND) specifications
   - Power cable connector specifications and installation warnings

6. **CloudEngine 16808 Power Supply System**: Supports DC, AC, and HVDC power supplies with dual power source capability through external power entry modules.

nodes.title: CloudEngine 16808 Power Supply System
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

## CloudEngine 16804 DC PEM
- Terminal block location mapping between PEM terminal blocks (A1/B1 through A6/B6) and Power Modules (PM 1-6)
- Terminal block descriptions for power cable connections, including NEG (-48V/-60V) and RTN (+) BGND terminal blocks with specifications for naked crimping connectors
- Safety notice regarding terminal bending

## CloudEngine 16808 Power Supply System Overview
- Supports DC, AC, and HVDC power supplies with external power distribution through a Power Entry Module (PEM)
- Dual power source support (A and B) with each Power Module (PM) having two inputs
- Power supply configuration guidelines for single and dual power sources, including load-balancing modes
- Automatic failover mechanisms and switching delays for AC/HVDC scenarios

## CloudEngine 16808 Components
- Power Modules (PM): Convert and distribute power through backplane and busbar
- Power Switches: Control PM output with mutual backup capability
- Power Entry Module (PEM): Connects to external power distribution with areas A and B for redundancy

## PM and PEM Applications
- Multiple power supply scenarios: DC (-48V), AC, 240V HVDC, and 380V HVDC
- Specific PEM and PM combinations for each scenario
- N+1 backup redundancy support with detailed configuration tables for DC and AC & HVDC PMs based on power consumption

## Power Supply Architecture
- DC power supply architecture with 20 inputs (10 per source A and B)
- AC & HVDC power supply architecture with PDU connectivity options (AC PDU and HVDC PDU)
- Three hybrid power supply modes illustrated with architectural diagrams

## CloudEngine 16808 AC & High-Voltage DC PEM
- Pre-installed module that cannot be removed, installed, or replaced onsite
- Connects the device to AC and high-voltage DC power sources

nodes.title: CloudEngine 16808 AC&High-Voltage DC PEM
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

1. **AC & HVDC Hybrid Power Supply Architecture**: Overview of the CloudEngine 16808's hybrid power supply system that supports both AC and high-voltage DC power sources.

2. **PEM (Power Entry Module) Overview**: Description of the CloudEngine 16808 AC & high-voltage DC PEM, which is pre-installed by default and cannot be removed, installed, or replaced onsite.

3. **Physical Appearance and Panel Layout**: Visual representation and description of the AC & high-voltage DC PEM's appearance and power socket configuration.

4. **Power Socket Mapping**: Detailed mapping between PEM power sockets (A1/B1 through A10/B10) and their corresponding power modules (1-10).

5. **Power Socket Specifications**: Technical specifications including supported voltage ranges (200V AC to 240V AC/240V DC/380V DC) and connector details (3-Pin, 450V, 16A, Straight Female, 1.5mm² or 14AWG).

6. **Input Requirements and Power Distribution**: Specifications for AC and DC power inputs, protection component requirements, rated current minimums (16A for UPS, 20A for mains), and power distribution frame (PDF) and power distribution box (PDB) configuration requirements.

7. **CloudEngine 16808 DC PEM**: Brief overview of the dedicated DC PEM module, which is also pre-installed and cannot be modified onsite.

nodes.title: CloudEngine 16808 DC PEM
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

- Support for 220V AC input in standard AC power distribution scenarios
- Support for 240V/380V high-voltage DC input in HVDC power distribution scenarios

- Requirements for configuring PDBs when front-end PDFs lack sufficient power inputs
- Minimum output current specifications based on power source (UPS vs. mains grid)
- Support for double-line backup capability
- Input current must meet or exceed the sum of connected output line currents

## CloudEngine 16808 DC PEM
- Overview of the DC Power Entry Module (PEM) for CloudEngine 16808 switches
- Pre-installed by default and cannot be removed, installed, or replaced onsite
- Physical appearance and terminal block layout specifications
- Mapping between PEM terminal blocks and Power Modules (PMs)
- Terminal block descriptions for negative (-48V/-60V) and return (BGND) connections
- Power cable connector specifications and installation warnings

## CloudEngine 16816 Power Supply System
- Support for DC, AC, and HVDC power supplies
- Connection through external power distribution system via PEMs
- Dual power source (A and B) support with dual inputs per power module

nodes.title: CloudEngine 16816 DC PEM
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

1. **AC PDB Specifications**: Output current requirements of at least 20A per line, support for double-line backup, and input current requirements matching the sum of connected output lines.

2. **CloudEngine 16816 DC PEM Overview**: Description of DC Power Entry Modules (PEMs) that connect the CloudEngine 16816 to DC power sources, noting they are pre-installed and cannot be removed or replaced onsite.

3. **DC PEM Configuration**: Explanation that CloudEngine 16816 and 16808 use the same DC PEM, with CloudEngine 16816 requiring two DC PEMs.

4. **DC PEM Physical Appearance and Terminal Blocks**: Visual representation and location mapping of terminal blocks on CloudEngine 16816 DC PEMs to Power Modules (PMs), with mapping details from A1/B1 through A20/B20 to PMs 1-20.

5. **Terminal Block Specifications**: Description of NEG (-) and RTN (+) terminal blocks with power cable connector specifications, including naked crimping connectors with different amperage ratings (80A and 160A) and a warning against bending terminals to 90 degrees onsite.

6. **Power Switch Functionality**: Description of dual backup switches that control power module output, with power enabled when one or both switches are in the ON state.

nodes.title: Power Switch
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

1. **Terminal Block Description** - Details of power cable terminals including NEG (-) and RTN (+) terminal blocks with specifications for naked crimping connectors (JG2, 25mm² and 35mm² variants) and a notice against bending terminals to 90 degrees onsite.

2. **Power Switch System** - Description of two backup switches that control power module output, with ON/OFF functionality. When one or both switches are ON, power modules output power and the device is powered on. When both are OFF, the device is powered off. A notice emphasizes both switches should be ON during device operation for reliability.

structure.title: Heat Dissipation System
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This section covers the heat dissipation system of CloudEngine 16800 series switches. The main points include:

1. **System Overview**: The heat dissipation system comprises fan modules and a chassis door designed to maintain optimal operating temperatures.

2. **Fan Module Functionality**: Multiple fan modules (FAN-240SA-B, FAN-480SA-B, FAN-960SA-B) are installed at the rear of the device and use front-to-back airflow to cool critical components including MPUs (Management Processing Units), LPUs (Line Processing Units), and SFUs (Switching Fabric Units).

3. **Power Module Cooling**: Power modules have integrated fans that independently manage heat dissipation through front-to-back airflow, ensuring power modules operate within normal temperature ranges.

4. **Chassis Door**: An optional chassis door installed at the front provides electromagnetic noise shielding and dust prevention to maintain proper chassis operation.

5. **Maintenance Requirements**: Vacant power module and card slots must be covered with filler panels to ensure efficient heat dissipation and electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) compliance.

6. **Card Heat Dissipation Design**: All cards utilize a front-to-back airflow design for effective thermal management.

nodes.title: Overview of the Heat Dissipation System
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This section of the CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches Hardware Description document covers the heat dissipation system. The main points include:

1. **System Overview**: The heat dissipation system comprises fan modules and a chassis door designed to maintain optimal operating temperatures for the switch components.

2. **Fan Module Function**: Fan modules installed at the rear of the device use front-to-back airflow to cool MPUs (Management Processing Units), LPUs (Line Processing Units), and SFUs (Switching Fabric Units). Power modules have built-in fans that independently manage their heat dissipation.

3. **Chassis Door**: An optional front-mounted chassis door that shields electromagnetic noise and prevents dust ingress while maintaining proper airflow.

4. **Heat Dissipation Design for Cards**: Details the three-stage airflow process where cold air enters through front panel air holes in MPUs and LPUs, flows through these components to SFUs, and hot air is exhausted by fan modules.

5. **Heat Dissipation Design for Power Modules**: Describes how power modules use built-in fans to draw cold air through their panels, with heated air exiting through the power backplane and PEM holes.

6. **Specific Fan Module Details**: Introduces the FAN-240SA-B fan module with its part number, model designation, and basic composition (fan tray, fans, and Fan Monitoring Unit).

7. **Important Notice**: Emphasizes the need to cover vacant slots with filler panels for efficient heat dissipation and EMC compliance.

# Description of Partial Document

This section provides a comprehensive overview of cards supported by CloudEngine 16800 series switches. The main points covered include:

1. **Card Classification and Types**: Cards are classified into three functional categories:
   - MPU (Main Processing Unit) - handles system control, management, and monitoring
   - LPU (Line Processing Unit) - responsible for data packet processing and traffic management
   - SFU (Switch Fabric Unit) - manages line-rate data switching in the data plane

2. **Card Series Variants**: Both LPUs and SFUs are available in three series:
   - G series
   - GK series
   - A series

3. **Specific Card Models**: Detailed listings of supported LPU models (such as CEL48XSFD-G, CEL24LQFD-G, etc.) and SFU models (such as CE-SFU04F-G, CE-SFU08G-GK, etc.) across all three series.

4. **Important Notices and Constraints**:
   - Active and standby MPU slots must contain the same type of MPU
   - Different types of LPUs or SFUs cannot be used together to avoid damage
   - Card availability is specified in product change notices (PCNs)
   - Color-coded labels identify card types near ejector levers

5. **Additional References**: The document mentions interface cards (10GE, 40GE, 100GE) and directs readers to version mapping information for detailed card compatibility details.

# Main Points of the Partial Document

This document section covers hardware specifications and port configuration details for CloudEngine 16800 series switches:

1. **Card Dimensions** - Defines how card height, width, and depth are measured on the front panel and connector areas.

2. **Port Numbering System** - Explains the hierarchical numbering format (stack member ID/slot ID/subcard ID/port number) used to identify ports, with specific slot ID ranges for different switch models (16804, 16808, 16816).

3. **Port Numbering Convention** - Describes how ports are physically numbered on cards from top to bottom and left to right, starting from 0, with an example of port naming (10GE 3/0/0).

4. **Port Splitting** - Details how higher-bandwidth ports (40GE, 100GE) can be split into multiple lower-bandwidth ports (10GE) with corresponding numbering conventions.

5. **Port Rate Description** - Covers various methods for determining and configuring port transmission rates:
   - Port Rate Auto-Sensing
   - Port Auto-Negotiation (allowing connected ports to exchange information and automatically configure transmission capabilities)
   - Port Split functionality
   - Manual Port Rate Configuration

6. **Supported Modules** - Lists available pluggable modules including 100GE, 40GE, 10GE, and GE optical modules, plus GE copper modules.

7. **MPU Introduction** - Begins documentation of the CE-MPUD-HALF (Main Processing Unit D half-width) component.

nodes.title: MPU
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

1. **Port Rate Auto-Sensing** - Allows ports to automatically operate at the rate of the installed medium without requiring manual command configuration.

2. **Port Auto-Negotiation** - Enables connected ports to exchange information and automatically negotiate port attributes (duplex mode, rate, and FEC) to operate at maximum supported transmission capability.

3. **Port Split** - Allows high-bandwidth physical ports to be divided into multiple independent lower-bandwidth ports, enabling flexible networking and reducing hardware costs (e.g., splitting a 40GE port into four 10GE ports).

4. **Port Rate Configuration** - Describes the ability to manually change interface rates through commands, with reference to detailed configuration guides.

5. **MPU (Main Processing Unit)** - Introduces the CE-MPUD-HALF module, which is the CE16800 Main Processing Unit D in half-width format, used in CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches.

nodes.summary:
# Description of CE-MPUF-HALF (CE16800 Main Processing Unit F - Half-Width)

This document section provides comprehensive technical documentation for the CE-MPUF-HALF, a half-width Main Processing Unit (MPU) card for CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches. The main points covered include:

## Basic Information
- Part numbers: 03050AVA and 03050AVA-001
- Card type: MPU (Main Processing Unit)
- Form factor: Half-width

## Compatibility and Version Mapping
- Supported products: CloudEngine 16804 and CloudEngine 16808
- Compatible interface cards with multiple models (CEL18CQFD, CEL24LQFD, CEL36CQFD, CEL48XSFD series)
- First supported version: V200R021C00SPC100 and later versions
- Available slot positions: 5-6 for CE16804, 9-10 for CE16808

## Physical Characteristics and Indicators
- Dimensions: 45.7 mm (H) x 215 mm (W) x 522.5 mm (D)
- Weight: 3.8 kg without packaging
- Multiple status indicators: Running status, Active/Standby status, Stack status, USB deployment, ETH port, SFP+ optical port, and high-precision clock indicators
- Control buttons: Reset button and OFL (power-off) button

## Ports and Connectivity
- Management ports: ETH (RJ45), Console (RJ45), USB 2.0
- Optical ports: Four SFP+ ports (10GE) for stack management
- Reserved ports: Clock SMA, ALMIN, RS485, CLK, TOD, FSP serial ports

## Functions and Features
- Device management and maintenance capabilities
- Device monitoring and remote management
- Out-of-band communication between cards
- Route calculation and routing protocol processing
- Data configuration and storage via SATA
- Hot swapping support

## Technical Specifications
- CPU: 8-core, 2.3 GHz
- Memory: 16 GB standard
- Storage: 32 GB SSD
- Power consumption: 117 W typical, 186 W maximum
- Heat dissipation: 400 BTU/hour typical, 635 BTU/hour maximum

nodes.summary:
# CE-SFU08G-A Switch Fabric Unit Documentation

This document section provides comprehensive technical information about the CE-SFU08G-A (CE16808 Switch Fabric Unit G-A), a critical component of CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches. The main points covered include:

## Basic Information and Identification
- Part number, model designation, and card type classification
- Physical appearance with mixed-card prevention labeling requirements

## Compatibility and Version Support
- Version mapping for CloudEngine 16808 product (V200R021C10SPC600 and later)
- Compatible slot locations (SFU3-SFU8)
- Compatibility matrices with interface cards (CEL18CQFD-A, CEL36CQFD-A, CEL36LQFD-A, CEL48XSFD-A)
- Compatible fan module specifications (FAN-480SA-B)

## Physical Indicators and Controls
- Running status indicator with color-coded states (green, red, yellow) and blinking patterns indicating operational status, faults, or power states
- OFL button for safe card removal (6-second hold requirement)
- Wrench removal button for mechanical extraction

## Functional Capabilities
- Line-rate data switching with six SFUs forming the switching core
- N+1 redundancy architecture for reliability and nonstop service
- Hot swapping support
- Restriction: Cannot be mixed with other SFU models in the same chassis

## Technical Specifications
- Physical dimensions and weight (with and without packaging)
- Power consumption metrics (typical, static, and maximum)
- Heat dissipation ratings
- Hardware specifications: Quad-core CPU at 1.4 GHz with 2 GB standard memory

This document provides comprehensive technical information about the CEL48XSFD-G, a 48-Port 10GE Interface Card for CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches. The main points covered include:

1. **Functions and Features**: Details on 10GE port capabilities supporting both 10GE and GE modes with various optical modules and cables, hot swapping support, and maximum port density specifications for different chassis models (16804, 16808, 16816).

2. **Technical Specifications**: Physical dimensions (with and without packaging), weight measurements, power consumption details (typical, static, and maximum), heat dissipation rates, CPU specifications (quad-core 1.4 GHz), and memory (4 GB standard).

3. **Basic Information**: Card identification including part number (03058873-003), model designation, and card type classification.

4. **Version Mapping**: Compatibility information showing supported CloudEngine products (16804, 16808, 16816) with first supported software versions and available slot numbers.

5. **Hardware Compatibility**: Detailed mapping tables showing compatibility with various MPU (Management Processing Unit) models and SFU (Switch Fabric Unit) models, including part numbers and version requirements.

6. **Indicators and Status**: Description of running status and port status indicators with color-coded meanings (green, yellow, red) and their operational states.

7. **Ports and Connectors**: Specification of 48 x 10GE optical ports with SFP+/SFP connectors and available component options.

nodes.title: CEL48XSFD-GK (48-port 10GE interface card (FD-GK, SFP+))
nodes.summary:
# Main Points of the Partial Document

This document describes the **CEL48XSFD-G network interface card**, covering:

## Functions and Features
- **10GE Port Capabilities**: Supports multiple optical module types and cable options depending on operating mode (10GE or GE mode)
- **Dual Mode Operation**: Can operate in 10GE mode with SFP+ modules or GE mode with SFP modules and copper connections
- **Auto-sensing**: 1000BASE-T copper modules automatically detect and support both 100 Mbit/s and 1000 Mbit/s speeds
- **Hot Swapping**: Supported for module replacement without downtime

## Technical Specifications
- **Physical Dimensions**: Detailed measurements provided both with and without packaging
- **Weight**: Specifications for packaged and unpackaged configurations
- **Power Consumption**: Three levels specified - typical (103W), static (85W), and maximum (193W)
- **Heat Dissipation**: Corresponding thermal output measurements in BTU/hour
- **Processing Power**: 4-core CPU running at 1.4 GHz
- **Memory**: Standard 4 GB configuration

The document is part of the CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches hardware documentation and specifically addresses a 48-port 10GE interface card model.

nodes.summary:
# Description of CEL48XSFD-GK 48-Port 10GE Interface Card

This document section provides comprehensive technical documentation for the CEL48XSFD-GK, a 48-port 10GE interface card for CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches. The main points covered include:

## Overview and Basic Information
- Part numbers: 03059594 and 03059594-001
- Card type: Interface Card for 10GE optical connectivity
- Compatibility with CloudEngine 16804, 16808, and 16816 chassis

## Version and Compatibility Mapping
- First supported version: V200R019C10 and later
- Compatible MPU models with specific part numbers and version requirements
- Compatible SFU models with detailed version mapping across different CloudEngine products

## Physical Characteristics and Indicators
- Appearance and physical layout documentation
- Running status indicator (RUN/ALM) with multiple states (green steady/blinking, red, yellow)
- Port status indicators showing link connectivity (LINK) and activity (ACT) for each of 48 ports

## Port Configuration
- 48 x 10GE optical ports with SFP+/SFP connectors
- Support for GE eSFP optical modules, GE SFP copper modules, 10GE SFP+ optical modules, and AOC cables
- Flexible mode operation: 10GE mode and GE mode with auto-sensing capabilities

## Functions, Features, and Technical Specifications
- Maximum port density: up to 768 10GE ports (CloudEngine 16816)
- Hot swapping support
- Detailed technical specifications including dimensions, weight, power consumption, heat dissipation, CPU (Quad-core 1.4 GHz), and 4GB standard memory
- Operational restrictions and remarks for specific software versions

structure.title: Cables
structure.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

This section describes the cables used in CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches, with a focus on DC Power Cables:

1. **Cable Types Overview**: The document lists nine types of cables including DC power cables, AC/HVDC power cables, ground cables, console cables, Ethernet cables, USB-to-Ethernet cables, fiber jumpers, high-speed cables, and AOC cables.

2. **DC Power Cables Composition**: DC power cables consist of –48 V power cables and RTN ground cables that must be cut to appropriate lengths on-site.

3. **Cable Selection Guidelines**: The document provides rules for choosing appropriate DC power cables based on connection scenarios:
   - For devices connected to a power distribution box or frame: use cables with JG2 90° right angle terminal at one end and OT terminal at the other end
   - For devices directly connected to a power distribution frame: use cables with JG2 90° right angle terminal at one end, with other terminal type and cable length determined by site survey results

4. **On-Site Customization**: Emphasis is placed on the need to customize cable lengths and configurations based on actual site conditions and survey results.

nodes.title: DC Power Cables
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

This section of the CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches Hardware Description document covers cable specifications and requirements, with primary focus on:

## Cable Types Overview
- Nine types of cables listed: DC Power Cables, AC&240V/380V HVDC Power Cable, Ground Cable, Console Cable, Ethernet Cable, USB-to-Ethernet Cable, Fiber Jumpers, High-Speed Cable, and AOC Cable

## DC Power Cables (Section 8.1)
- **Composition**: –48V power cables and RTN ground cables
- **Terminal Types**: JG2 90° right angle terminals and OT terminals
- **Cross-sectional Areas**: 25 mm² and 35 mm² available
- **Cable Length Specifications**: Based on power module output (2200W max) and rated input current (46A), with maximum lengths of 27m (25mm²) and 38m (35mm²)

## Connection Scenarios
- Device connected to power distribution box or frame
- Device directly connected to power distribution frame

## Technical Specifications
- Detailed tables listing part numbers, models, specifications, and fireproof levels for:
  - 25 mm² power cables (multiple colors and regional variants)
  - 35 mm² power cables (multiple colors and regional variants)
  - Power cable terminals for both connection types
  - Compliance with international standards (VDE, UL, IEC, BIS)

## AC&240V/380V HVDC Power Cable (Section 8.2)
- Requires Huawei-customized HVDC connectors
- Only Huawei-delivered cables are compatible

nodes.title: Ground Cable
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

1. **HVDC Power Cable Specifications** - Details of a 2.5m power cable (Part Number 04151963) with 3*1.5mm² conductors, including connector types (PI angle male and HVDC 3 straight female) and technical specifications for China region.

2. **Ground Cable Overview and Purpose** - Explanation that ground cables ensure equipotential between chassis and cabinet, protecting equipment from lightning and electromagnetic interference.

3. **Ground Cable Physical Characteristics** - Description of the ground cable's appearance and internal structure, with identification of the ground point location on the chassis (rear right side with yellow grounding label).

4. **Ground Cable Connection Method** - Instructions for proper connection showing that OT bare crimp terminal X1 connects to the chassis ground point and terminal X2 connects to the cabinet ground bar.

5. **Ground Cable Technical Specifications** - A detailed table listing three ground cable variants with different lengths (0.85m, 2m, and 4m), all featuring OT2-25-8 and OT25-6 connectors with H07Z-K-25² G&Y specifications.

nodes.title: Console Cable
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

This document section describes three types of cables used with CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches:

## Ground Cable
- **Purpose**: Protects devices from lightning and electromagnetic interference
- **Connection**: OT bare crimp terminals connect to chassis ground point (X1) and cabinet ground bar (X2)
- **Specifications**: Available in three lengths (0.85m, 2m, 4m) with specific part numbers and technical specifications detailed in a specifications table

## Console Cable
- **Purpose**: Connects the console port of a switch to a serial port for device commissioning and local maintenance
- **Structure**: Includes DB9 female connector and 8-pin RJ45 connector
- **Pin Assignments**: Details provided for RS232_RX, RS232_TX, and GND connections between DB9 (X1) and RJ45 (X2) connectors
- **Flexibility**: Can use shielded or unshielded cables depending on site requirements

## Ethernet Cable
- **Types**: Classified into two categories:
  - Straight-through cables with specific RJ45 pin assignments
  - Crossover cables with different RJ45 pin assignments
- **Characteristics**: Standard unshielded twisted pairs using RJ45 connectors; both types have identical appearance

nodes.title: Ethernet Cable
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

This document section describes various types of cables used with CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches:

1. **Console Cable (DB9/RJ45)**: Explains the connection between a switch's console port and a maintenance terminal, with pin assignments for RS232_TX and GND connections using either shielded or unshielded cables.

2. **Ethernet Cables**: Covers two main types:
   - **Straight-through cables**: Used to connect terminals (PCs, switches) to network devices, with detailed pin assignments showing wire colors and corresponding pin numbers on both ends
   - **Crossover cables**: Used to connect two terminals together, with different pin assignments than straight-through cables

3. **Cable Structure and Appearance**: Provides visual references and descriptions of Ethernet cable appearance and internal structure, noting that both straight-through and crossover cables look identical despite different pin configurations.

4. **Technical Specifications**: Includes pin assignment tables for both cable types and notes on proper wire pairing (pins 1-2 and 3-6) for optimal electrical transmission performance.

5. **USB-to-Ethernet Cable**: Introduces a cable type with a USB port on one end and two RJ45 connectors on the other end, with different pin assignments for standard and non-standard console ports.

nodes.title: Types of Optical Modules
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This section of a hardware description document covers optical modules used in CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches. The main points include:

## Types of Optical Modules Classification

**By Transmission Rates:** Optical modules are categorized as 100GE, 40GE, 25GE, 10GE, FE, and GE modules based on their transmission speeds.

**By Encapsulation Types:** Nine types are described (SFP, eSFP, SFP+, XFP, SFP28, QSFP+, CXP, CFP, QSFP28), each with specific characteristics, connector types, and use cases. All modules are hot-swappable.

**By Physical Layer Standards:** Different standards enable data transmission in various modes.

**By Fiber Modes:** Modules are classified into single-mode (for long-distance, high-capacity transmission) and multi-mode (for short-distance, lower-cost transmission), with specific guidelines on optical splitter usage and link budget considerations.

**Wavelength Division Multiplexing:** CWDM and DWDM modules transmit different center wavelengths on a single fiber, enabling long-distance transmission with reduced fiber requirements.

## Key Considerations

- Long-distance optical modules require careful fiber length selection to prevent receiver overload
- Optical attenuators may be needed for short fiber runs
- Single-mode and multi-mode optical splitters have specific limitations based on IEEE standards

## Optical Module Terms

The document defines key terminology including transmission distance and interface rate specifications.

nodes.title: Optical Module Terms
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

## Optical Multiplexing and Long-Distance Transmission
- DWDM and CWDM modules enable multiple lights with different wavelengths to be transmitted on a single fiber without interference
- Passive multiplexers and demultiplexers reduce the number of optical fibers required
- Long-distance optical modules have transmit power that often exceeds overload power, requiring careful fiber length selection and use of optical attenuators to prevent receiver damage

## Optical Module Terminology
The document defines key technical terms including:
- **Transmission distance**: Maximum distance optical signals can travel
- **Interface rate**: Maximum electrical signal transmission rate (125 Mbit/s to 41.25 Gbit/s)
- **Encapsulation types**: Various physical form factors (SFP, SFP+, XFP, QSFP+, CFP, etc.) with different specifications and applications
- **Center wavelength**: Midpoint wavelength in the transmit spectrum
- **Fiber mode**: Single-mode vs. multi-mode fibers with different dispersion and distance capabilities
- **Modal bandwidth**: Spectrum characteristics measurement
- **Fiber diameter and class**: Standards for different fiber types (G.651, G.652, G.653, G.655)
- **Connector types**: LC and MPO connectors for different module types
- **Power specifications**: Transmit optical power, receiver sensitivity, and overload optical power
- **Extinction ratio**: Signal identification capability

## Optical Module Installation Guidelines
- OTDR testing requires fiber removal to prevent receiver damage
- Single-mode optical splitters should be avoided; if necessary, strict link budget compliance is required
- Multi-mode optical modules are unreliable with optical splitters
- Optical path checking methods are referenced

nodes.title: Guide to Using Optical Modules
nodes.summary:
# Main Points of the Partial Document

This document section covers optical fiber and optical module specifications and maintenance procedures for CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches:

## Key Topics:

1. **Fiber Classes** - Four types of optical fibers (G.651, G.652, G.653, G.655) with different wavelength and dispersion characteristics

2. **Connector Types** - LC and MPO connectors used in various optical modules (SFP, SFP+, XFP, QSFP+, CFP, CXP)

3. **Optical Module Performance Parameters** - Transmit optical power, receiver sensitivity, overload optical power, and extinction ratio specifications

4. **Installation Precautions** - Guidelines for safe installation including OTDR testing procedures, single-mode and multi-mode optical splitter considerations

5. **Optical Path Checking Method** - Four-step procedure involving module/fiber removal, end face inspection, patch cord verification, and insertion loss/reflection testing

6. **Optical Fiber Connector Standards** - Maximum attenuation requirements (0.3 dB for splicing/mechanical connectors, 0.75 dB for optical connectors) per ANSI/TIA-568-C and GBT50312-2016 standards

7. **Connector Loss and Reflectance Testing** - Systematic troubleshooting procedure using OTDR measurements to identify and resolve abnormal ports through cleaning, connector tightening, flange replacement, or fiber replacement

8. **Optical Module Link Cleaning** - Required tools and accessories for maintaining optical module end faces to prevent contamination-related issues

nodes.title: Optical Module Link Cleaning
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

## Optical Fiber Connector Testing and Troubleshooting
- Testing procedures for optical fiber connectors using OTDR meters to verify loss and reflectance values
- Acceptable thresholds: fiber splicing connector loss <0.3 dB, connector loss <0.75 dB, connector reflection <-30 dB
- Step-by-step troubleshooting process including port location identification, cleaning, and replacement procedures for flanges and optical fibers
- Handling of multiple abnormal points on links

## Optical Module Link Cleaning
- Importance of cleaning optical module and fiber end faces to prevent contamination-related issues (dust, oil) that can cause abnormal optical power, bit errors, or link interruption
- Required tools and accessories including ESD protection equipment, cleaning pens, wipes, and end face detectors
- ESD control requirements and safety precautions during cleaning operations
- Detailed cleaning procedures for optical module end faces using lint-free swabs or module cleaning pens
- Cleaning procedures for optical fiber end faces using module cleaning pens or fiber cleaning wipes
- Proper techniques for cleaning, including directional wiping, appropriate swab sizes for different port types (SC, LC, MTRJ), and reuse limitations
- Post-cleaning verification using end face detectors and restoration procedures

## Optical Module Interoperation Rules
- Standards-based interoperability guidelines for optical modules with the same specifications
- Standards define rate, wavelength, and transmission distance but not encapsulation modes

nodes.title: Rules for Optical Module Interoperation
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

1. **Optical Fiber Cleaning Procedures**
   - Two methods for cleaning optical fiber end faces: using lint-free swabs and using fiber cleaning wipes
   - Step-by-step cleaning instructions for each method
   - Use of end face detector to verify cleanliness after cleaning
   - Adapter cleaning procedures using lint-free swabs

2. **Cleaning Best Practices and Cautions**
   - Proper technique for wiping (one direction only, along the grain)
   - Use of anhydrous alcohol to improve cleaning effectiveness
   - Importance of maintaining vertical alignment during cleaning
   - Single-use policy for cleaning wipes and swabs

3. **Fiber Restoration and Link Verification**
   - Proper insertion procedure for cleaned fibers into optical modules
   - Verification of link status, optical power, bit errors, and alarms
   - Troubleshooting steps if faults persist (patch cord/module replacement, OTDR testing)

4. **Optical Module Interoperation Rules**
   - Standards for optical module compatibility and interoperability
   - Definition of optical module standards using rate, wavelength, transmission distance, and encapsulation modes
   - Explanation of standard naming conventions (e.g., 1000BASE-LX10)
   - Standards organizations (IEEE 802.3, SFF, MSAs)

5. **Copper Modules Overview**
   - Introduction to RJ45 copper modules as an alternative to optical modules
   - Key difference: copper modules do not perform electrical-optical conversion

nodes.title: Understanding Copper Modules
nodes.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

1. **Optical Module Interoperability** - Instructions for achieving interoperability between optical modules with different standards and compliance requirements when connecting products.

2. **Optical Module Standards Nomenclature** - Detailed explanation of standard naming conventions using 1000BASE-LX10 as an example, including:
   - Data transmission rate (1000 Mbit/s, 10 Mbit/s, 100 Mbit/s, etc.)
   - Baseband transmission indicator (BASE)
   - Laser center wavelength designations (S, L, E, B)
   - Encoding formats (T, X, R, W)
   - Number of channels (1, 4, or 10)

3. **Standards Organizations** - Overview of organizations that define optical module standards:
   - IEEE 802.3 for MAC and PHY standards
   - Small Form Factor (SFF) committee and Multi-Source Agreements (MSAs) for hardware, software, and structure standards

4. **Copper Modules (RJ45 Modules)** - Introduction to copper modules as an alternative to optical modules, explaining that they do not perform electrical-optical conversion and can be connected using copper cables.

5. **GE eSFP Optical Modules** - Brief introduction to specific optical module types, including the eSFP-GE-SX-MM850 model with reference to its appearance.

   - Transmission rate: 1 Gbit/s

3. **Transmitter Optical Characteristics**

4. **Receiver Optical Characteristics**
   - Receiver sensitivity: -23 dBm
   - Overload power: -3.0 dBm

5. **Matching Optical Fiber Specifications**

   - End face type: PC/UPC
   - Core size: 9/125 μm

6. **Additional Features**
   - DDM (Digital Diagnostic Monitoring) supported
   - Single-fiber bidirectional operation requiring paired modules

# Description of Partial Document

This document section provides detailed technical specifications for the **SFP-10G-SRV-MP optical module**, a pluggable interface module for CloudEngine 16800 Series Switches. The main points covered include:

## Basic Information
- Module identification details including part number (02313BJJ) and form factor (SFP+)
- 10GBASE-SR optical port standard with LC/DLC connector type
- Multimode fiber (MMF) support with PC/UPC end face type
- Operating temperature range: 0°C to 70°C
- Transmission rate: 10 Gbit/s

## Transmission Specifications
- Target transmission distances ranging from 26m to 400m depending on fiber type (OM1 through OM4)
- Modal bandwidth specifications for different multimode fiber types (160 MHz*km to 4700 MHz*km)

## Optical Characteristics
- **Transmitter**: Center wavelength of 850 nm, maximum Tx power of -1.0 dBm, minimum Tx power of -7.3 dBm, extinction ratio of 3.0 dB
- **Receiver**: Rx sensitivity of -11.1 dBm, overload power of -1.0 dBm

## Features and Compatibility
- DDM (Digital Diagnostic Monitoring) support enabled
- Matching optical fiber specifications with core size of 50/125μm
- No port splitting capability
